MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-05-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER DIMITRUS FORAKIS' PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM CLAIM REQUIREMENT [GOV. CODE SEC. 946.6]. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. PETITIONER HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF PROVING, BY A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE, THAT THE FAILURE TO FILE A TIMELY CLAIM WAS DUE TO MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. (SANTEE V. SANTA CLARA COUNTY OFFICE OF EDUC. (1990) 220 CAL.APP.3D 702, 717.) IN ORDER TO SHOW THAT THE MISTAKE OR NEGLECT WAS EXCUSABLE, PETITIONER MUST PROVE THAT THE FAILURE OCCURRED DESPITE AN EXERCISE OF DILIGENCE. (DEPARTMENT OF WATER & POWER V. SUPERIOR COURT (2000) 82 CAL.APP.4TH 1288, 1293.) SIMILAR TO THE ATTORNEY IN TACKETT V. CITY OF HUNTINGTON BEACH, PETITIONER'S ATTORNEY HAS PROVIDED INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO EXPLAIN HIS CLAIM OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT OR TO DEMONSTRATE THAT HE PURSUED PETITIONER'S CASE WITH DILIGENCE. (SEE TACKETT V. CITY OF HUNTINGTON BEACH (1994) 22 CAL.APP.4TH 60.) ADDITIONALLY, PETITIONER'S RELIANCE UPON VILES V. STATE OF CALIFORNIA (1967) 66 CAL.2D 24 IS MISPLACED BECAUSE THAT CASE'S FACTS ARE DISTINGUISHABLE FROM THOSE IN THE INSTANT CASE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

calendar.matters:
Petition For Relief From Claim Requirement [Gov. Code Sec. 946.6]
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER DIMITRUS FORAKIS' Petition For Relief From Claim Requirement [Gov. Code Sec. 946.6] is denied. Petitioner has not met his burden of proving, by a preponderance of the evidence, that the failure to file a timely claim was due to mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. (Santee v. Santa Clara County Office of Educ. (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 702, 717.) In order to show that the mistake or neglect was excusable, petitioner must prove that the failure occurred despite an exercise of diligence. (Department of Water & Power v. Superior Court (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1288, 1293.) Similar to the attorney in Tackett v. City of Huntington Beach, Petitioner's attorney has provided insufficient evidence to explain his claim of excusable neglect or to demonstrate that he pursued Petitioner's case with diligence. (See Tackett v. City of Huntington Beach (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 60.) Additionally, Petitioner's reliance upon Viles v. State of California (1967) 66 Cal.2d 24 is misplaced because that case's facts are distinguishable from those in the instant case. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/EG

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER DIMITRUS FORAKIS' Petition For A Relief From Claim Requirement - The matter is continued to November 16, 2015, to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 16, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/ehg
